skill.name: grammar-style-enhancer
skill.description: Analyzes prose for grammar errors, style inconsistencies, clarity issues, and readability problems. Provides specific suggestions for improvement while preserving the author's unique voice. Use when the user needs help polishing their writing, improving clarity, or maintaining consistent style.
skill.summary: Reviews prose for grammar, clarity, style, readability, and voice-preserving improvements.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives from markdown fences and writing-instruction prose, with no executable code, shell invocation, network activity, credential access, or prompt-injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick execution findings are markdown fences around example reports. They do not invoke a shell, execute Ruby, call a subprocess, or accept user-controlled command input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are fenced markdown delimiters for sample output. The skill contains only SKILL.md prose and no executable command runner.

low_findings.title: Writing Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives caused by ordinary writing-analysis vocabulary. The skill discusses weak verbs, stronger wording, paragraph rhythm, and similar editorial concepts, not hashing or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose about writing quality and sample revisions. I found no cryptographic APIs, hash functions, encryption routines, or security-sensitive data handling.

low_findings.title: Editorial Search Terms Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings are false positives from editorial instructions such as search, find, reading level, citations, and word-choice review. They do not inspect the host system or network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms appear in grammar and style advice. There are no commands such as host discovery, port scanning, DNS enumeration, or network requests.

content.value_statement: Drafts often contain unclear sentences, grammar errors, and inconsistent style choices. This skill gives structured editing feedback that improves clarity while preserving the author's voice.
actual_capabilities: Checks prose for grammar, punctuation, tense, and agreement issues.
Identifies passive voice, weak verbs, filler words, and repetitive sentence patterns.
Provides before-and-after rewrite options with explanations.
Assesses readability, sentence variety, paragraph flow, and tone fit.
Adapts feedback for fiction, nonfiction, academic, and professional writing.
Prioritizes edits by urgency, impact, and voice preservation.
limitations: It does not fact-check claims, sources, or citation accuracy.
It depends on the user's genre, audience, and style preferences.
It may suggest edits that the author should reject for voice or intent.
It is not a replacement for a professional editor on high-stakes documents.

use_cases.title: Polish Fiction Drafts
use_cases.description: Review scenes for stronger verbs, clearer emotion, better pacing, and fewer repeated sentence patterns.

use_cases.title: Improve Academic Tone
use_cases.description: Convert informal phrasing into clearer academic prose with stronger claims and cleaner structure.

use_cases.title: Refine Business Writing
use_cases.description: Make reports, proposals, and emails more concise, consistent, and easy to scan.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Grammar Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for a short passage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this text for grammar, punctuation, and clarity. Keep my original tone where possible: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Style and Voice Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review focused on prose quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this passage for weak verbs, passive voice, repetition, and sentence rhythm. Suggest improvements that preserve my voice: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Specific Rewrite
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted editing for a defined audience.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this for [audience] in a [formal/casual/academic/literary] style. Explain the most important changes: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Full Editorial Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review with priorities and examples.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full grammar and style enhancement report for this draft. Include priorities, examples, rewrite options, and voice preservation notes: [paste text]

output_examples.input: Sarah was walking down the hallway. The hallway was very dark. She was scared.
output_examples.output: The report flags repeated structure, weak verbs, and telling. It suggests a tighter version such as Sarah walked through the dark hallway, breath catching as each step echoed.

output_examples.input: Many researchers think that climate change is a really big problem.
output_examples.output: The report identifies informal wording and vague claims. It suggests more precise academic phrasing and reminds the writer to add a reliable citation.

output_examples.input: Please review my proposal introduction for clarity and tone.
output_examples.output: The report highlights unclear claims, long sentences, and inconsistent formality. It recommends concise wording and a clearer opening structure.

best_practices: Provide the genre, audience, and desired tone before asking for edits.
Review suggestions in passes, starting with correctness and clarity.
Keep edits that support your intent and reject changes that weaken your voice.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for every sentence to be rewritten without explaining your goal.
Do not accept all style changes automatically.
Do not use generated citations or factual claims without independent verification.

faq.question: Can this skill rewrite my entire draft?
faq.answer: It can help revise sections, but shorter chunks produce more accurate and useful feedback.

faq.question: Will it preserve my writing voice?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow asks for voice preferences and explains edits so you can choose what to keep.

faq.question: Can it handle academic writing?
faq.answer: Yes, it can improve tone, precision, structure, and clarity for academic passages.

faq.question: Does it check citations?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest where citations may be needed, but it does not verify source accuracy.

faq.question: Can it identify passive voice and weak verbs?
faq.answer: Yes. It flags those patterns and suggests stronger alternatives when appropriate.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-native English writers?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains grammar and style issues in direct language with specific examples.
